Politics.headline: Trump China visit live: US president says a lot of problems 'settled', as he meets with Xi on final day of summit
Politics.detail: President Trump stated that many issues were 'settled' as he concluded his summit with Xi Jinping in China, although details of any agreements remain unclear. The meetings focused on trade and ongoing tensions, including the situation with Iran.

Politics.headline: Streeting Resigns
Politics.detail: Britain's Health Secretary, Wes Streeting, resigned from the Labour government, deepening a crisis for Prime Minister Keir Starmer; Labour lawmaker Josh Simons also resigned, potentially allowing Andy Burnham to challenge Starmer.

Politics.headline: Supreme Court Maintains Widespread Access to Abortion Pills
Politics.detail: The Supreme Court preserved access to a common abortion pill, allowing doctors to continue mailing the drug to patients without an in-person visit; Justices Thomas and Alito dissented. This maintains the status quo while legal challenges continue.

Politics.headline: US Border Patrol chief Michael Banks is resigning, in latest DHS leadership change
Politics.detail: US Border Patrol chief Michael Banks is resigning amid a series of leadership changes within the Department of Homeland Security. The reasons for his departure were not immediately clear.

Politics.headline: House Republicans Block Resolution to Halt Iran War
Politics.detail: House Republicans narrowly blocked a resolution that would have required Trump to seek congressional approval to continue military operations against Iran, revealing divisions within the parties. The vote ended in a tie due to a few Republicans supporting the measure and one Democrat opposing it.

Finance & Economy.headline: British Gas faces record £112m settlement over prepayment meter scandal
Finance & Economy.detail: British Gas is expected to pay a record £112 million settlement due to issues related to prepayment meters.

Finance & Economy.headline: Tesco boss's pay rises by more than £1m to £10.8m after market share surge
Finance & Economy.detail: The pay for the boss of Tesco has increased by over £1 million, reaching £10.8 million following a surge in market share.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's forex reserves jump by $6.295 billion to $696.988 billion
Finance & Economy.detail: India's foreign exchange reserves have increased by $6.295 billion, reaching a total of $696.988 billion.

Finance & Economy.headline: NY Empire State Manufacturing Index unexpectedly increased to 19.6 in May 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The NY Empire State Manufacturing Index in the US unexpectedly increased to 19.6 in May 2026 from 11 in April, above forecasts of 7.5.

Finance & Economy.headline: Retail investors' shift towards AI chip stocks could signal an end to the boom
Finance & Economy.detail: A shift by retail investors towards AI chip stocks might indicate a potential end to the current boom.

Technology.headline: Meta is bringing third-party apps and games to its display glasses
Technology.detail: The $800 smart glasses could soon be a lot more useful with the addition of third-party apps and games.

Technology.headline: Razer updates the Blade 18 with new chips, a specced-out model costs $7,000
Technology.detail: The 2026 model adds the Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor, offering enhanced performance for demanding users.

Technology.headline: Spotify and its hosting platforms are adopting Apple's tech for video podcasts
Technology.detail: HTTP Live Streaming support will let Apple Podcasts play Spotify-hosted video podcasts, expanding accessibility.

Technology.headline: AMD will bring its next-generation FSR 4.1 upscaling tech to older GPUs
Technology.detail: RDNA 3 players can start using it in over 300 games this July, improving performance on older hardware.

Technology.headline: This new Claude skill saves you from bad contracts - and costs less than a lawyer
Technology.detail: A new Claude skill has been developed to help users avoid problematic contracts at a lower cost than hiring a lawyer.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover Giant “Last Titan” Dinosaur, Southeast Asia's Largest Ever
Science.detail: A massive new dinosaur discovered in Thailand, named Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, is rewriting Southeast Asia's prehistoric history, weighing around 27 tonnes and living more than 100 million years ago; it may be the last giant sauropod to roam the region before rising seas transformed the landscape.

Science.headline: Scientists discover vitamin B2 may help cancer cells survive
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that vitamin B2 may have a surprising dark side, potentially helping cancer cells to survive by supporting a cellular shield that protects tumors from ferroptosis, a form of programmed cell death linked to cancer suppression. In lab tests, researchers used a vitamin B2-like compound called roseoflavin to break down that protection and trigger cancer cell death.

Science.headline: Coal pollution is cutting solar power output, study finds
Science.detail: New research indicates that pollution from coal-fired power plants is significantly reducing the energy output of solar photovoltaic installations, especially where they are located near each other. Aerosols, or tiny particles in the air, reduced global solar electricity output by 5.8% in 2023, which is equivalent to 111 terawatt-hours of lost energy.

Science.headline: Scientists are reading Block Island's past to protect its future
Science.detail: Two new URI studies will decode over a century of coastal change on Block Island, delivering planning tools and scientific guidance directly to the community, backed by more than $800,000 in combined Rhode Island Sea Grant funding and matching funds. The studies will create a detailed picture of how Block Island's shoreline and salt marshes are changing and what is driving those changes.

Science.headline: Scientists Stunned As Volcano Removes Methane From the Air
Science.detail: A giant volcano may have accidentally uncovered a powerful new way to destroy methane in the air. Unusual helium signatures detected in Zambian hot springs may signal that a hidden tectonic rift is forming.

Health.headline: New Ebola outbreak confirmed in remote Congo province, with 65 deaths recorded
Health.detail: Africa CDC confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths recorded.

Health.headline: Menopause Symptoms Worse in Women With Abdominal Obesity
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that women experiencing menopause with abdominal obesity may face more severe symptoms.

Health.headline: Few Pediatric Cancer Drugs Reach Phase 3 or Approval
Health.detail: A new report highlights the challenges in pediatric oncology drug development, noting that few drugs for childhood cancers progress to advanced clinical trials or regulatory approval.

Health.headline: Brain Cooling Boosts LVO Recovery After Thrombectomy
Health.detail: Research suggests that therapeutic brain cooling can improve recovery outcomes for patients with large vessel occlusion (LVO) following thrombectomy.

Health.headline: License To Deliver: Some Midwives Break the Law To Assist With Home Births
Health.detail: A report examines how some midwives operate outside legal frameworks to assist with home births, raising questions about regulation and access to care.

Sports.headline: Kevin Walters signs extension with Kangaroos, will coach Australia in 2026 World Cup
Sports.detail: Kevin Walters will lead the Australian Kangaroos through the 2026 Rugby League World Cup after signing a two-year extension with the ARLC; his contract runs through June 2028.

Sports.headline: Swans claim thriller after stunning 'tidal wave' as 'disastrous' blow rocks Pies
Sports.detail: Sydney survived a big Collingwood scare with an epic second-half comeback, as Brodie Grundy dominated after Magpies ruckman Oscar Steene suffered a knee injury; Sydney won 11.15 (81) to 10.15 (75).

Sports.headline: Browns' 2026 Schedule Opens in Florida
Sports.detail: The Cleveland Browns will begin the 2026 NFL season with their first two games in Florida, facing the Jaguars in Jacksonville on September 13th and the Buccaneers in Tampa Bay the following week.

Sports.headline: PBA: Rain or Shine, Ginebra aim to buck twice-to-beat trend in deciders
Sports.detail: Rain or Shine and Ginebra are aiming to defy the twice-to-beat advantage in their respective PBA deciders, as they seek to overcome the odds and secure a victory.

Sports.headline: Mitchell Kernels softball fell 2–1 in a walk-off loss at Pierre on Thursday
Sports.detail: Mitchell Kernels softball team lost 2-1 to Pierre after a walk-off, despite taking an early lead; Rylee Jennings had nine strikeouts.

Entertainment.headline: Barbara Palvin, Dylan Sprouse reveal pregnancy with first child
Entertainment.detail: Model Barbara Palvin and actor Dylan Sprouse announced they are expecting their first child.

Entertainment.headline: Nico Waje faces separate complaint filed by another woman
Entertainment.detail: Another woman has filed a complaint against Nico Waje.

Entertainment.headline: Eurovision contestants set to compete in 2026 Grand Final — here's what to watch for
Entertainment.detail: The Eurovision Grand Final is set to take place in Vienna, Austria, with 25 acts competing for the title, drawing in tens of millions of viewers. Expect polished pop, theatrical staging, and a 50/50 jury-and-public vote.

Entertainment.headline: Drake 'froze' the CN Tower in honour of his new album, Iceman
Entertainment.detail: Drake celebrated the release of his new album, 'Iceman,' by doing something special with the CN Tower.

Entertainment.headline: Shakira and Burna Boy release official 2026 FIFA World Cup Anthem, 'Dai Dai'
Entertainment.detail: Shakira and Burna Boy have released 'Dai Dai,' the official anthem for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

Education.headline: Adults locked out of learning to access education with new reform
Education.detail: The government has announced the first 130 universities and colleges approved to offer new bite-sized courses, with applications opening this September, providing a new route into university for adults balancing work and childcare. From September 2026, people will be able to access student finance for shorter, flexible courses, known as 'modules', as well as traditional degrees.

Education.headline: Education leaders, advocates weigh in on RCSD's passed budget for 2026-27
Education.detail: The Rochester city school board passed a $1.16 billion budget, though the district is anticipating a fiscal cliff in the coming years, leading to skepticism and doubt from community members, staff, and parents. The budget includes cuts to positions like guidance counselors and social workers but adds over 100 full-time positions in special education.

Education.headline: Colorado lawmakers debated education bills over teacher housing, state testing and more. Here's where they landed.
Education.detail: Colorado lawmakers considered nearly 60 education bills, including one to codify the civil rights of students with disabilities into state law, but a bill aimed at expanding rental housing for teachers was postponed indefinitely. The legislation spells out provisions to ensure students with disabilities have equal access to education and don't face discrimination.

Education.headline: Imagining meaningful cooperation: A philanthropic perspective
Education.detail: A new vision for international education cooperation argues that philanthropy, governments and multilaterals must build adaptive, locally led networks to achieve SDG4. The global education community is fragmented, and international cooperation for education is under-resourced, requiring actors who understand government diplomacy and private capital.

Education.headline: NTA announces NEET-UG re-exam on June 21
Education.detail: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has announced that the medical entrance exam NEET-UG, which was cancelled due to alleged irregularities, will be re-conducted on June 21. The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) will conduct a comprehensive inquiry into the alleged irregularities.

Lifestyle.headline: Experience: I smuggled myself out of the UK
Lifestyle.detail: A person recounts their experience of smuggling themselves out of the UK.

Lifestyle.headline: Instant noodle recall issued nationwide over possible peanut contamination
Lifestyle.detail: A nationwide recall has been issued for instant noodles due to potential peanut contamination.

Lifestyle.headline: Papa John's partners with Alphabet's Wing to test drone delivery
Lifestyle.detail: Papa John's is partnering with Alphabet's Wing to explore and test drone delivery services.

Lifestyle.headline: Demi Moore bends the Cannes dress rules in Gucci
Lifestyle.detail: Demi Moore made waves at Cannes, bending dress code expectations while wearing Gucci.

Lifestyle.headline: Mschf designs kit, capsule for OTB-backed soccer club L.R. Vicenza
Lifestyle.detail: Mschf has created a kit and capsule collection for the OTB-backed soccer club L.R. Vicenza.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Acknowledge Hamas crimes
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Toledo Blade editorial board calls on Palestinian activists to acknowledge the brutality of the Hamas attackers on Oct. 7, 2023, citing a well-documented report of sexual violence against Israeli women and others.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on a cabinet resignation: Labour's leadership crisis is really an identity crisis
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian analyzes the Labour party's recent cabinet resignation, arguing that it highlights a deeper identity crisis within the party.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on life after Orbán: Péter Magyar's fast start bodes well for Hungary and for Europe
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian expresses optimism about Hungary and Europe following Péter Magyar's promising start in the post-Orbán era.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Women! There is power in numbers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Elizabeth Keifer writes in the CT Mirror about the power women have when they unite and take action against current issues such as climate change, racism, and sexism.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Power outages, water service cutoffs: The scourge of summer
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Manila Times editorial board discusses the issues of power outages and water service cutoffs that are plaguing the summer season.

World.headline: Trump-Xi summit: China, US disagree on what they agreed on
World.detail: The US touted trade deals; China said it warned Washington over Taiwan, and neither side confirmed the other's claims.

World.headline: Philippines vows to hand fugitive senator to ICC following shootout
World.detail: The Philippines is promising to hand over a fugitive senator to the ICC following a shootout.

World.headline: Deadly attack on Pakistan outpost puts Afghanistan ceasefire at risk
World.detail: A deadly attack on a Pakistan outpost is endangering the Afghanistan ceasefire.

World.headline: Health officials raise alarm over new Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
World.detail: Health officials are raising the alarm about a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

World.headline: Ukraine attacks Russia with drones after suffering three days of massive strikes
World.detail: Ukraine has launched drone attacks on Russia following three days of significant strikes against them.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa Quietly Departs For Belarus Ahead Of Malaba Farewell
Zimbabwe.detail: President Emmerson Mnangagwa left for Belarus without ceremony, missing the farewell for retiring Chief Justice Luke Malaba.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare Records No Confirmed Hantavirus Cases: City Council
Zimbabwe.detail: The Harare City Council announced that there are no confirmed cases of Hantavirus in Harare.

Zimbabwe.headline: South Africa applauds Zimbabwe's Beitbridge Border upgrade plans
Zimbabwe.detail: South Africa has commended Zimbabwe for its plans to upgrade the Beitbridge Border Post.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabweans lured into Russia's war as families plead for their return
Zimbabwe.detail: Families are appealing for the return of Zimbabweans who have been lured into fighting in the war in Russia.

Zimbabwe.headline: Freight costs threaten Zimbabwe farmers' livelihoods as Iran war disrupts exports
Zimbabwe.detail: Rising freight costs due to the Iran war are posing a threat to Zimbabwean farmers' livelihoods and the horticulture export sector, despite record exports last year.
